fields.description:
## Why
`CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` is exposed as an env var ONLY in the remote/cloud runner; the
LOCAL Claude Code CLI does not export it (unshipped feature request). Without a shim the
feature silently no-ops for local-CLI users — the most common case. The robust, harness-
agnostic capture (mirroring claude-mem) is a SessionStart hook that reads the hook's
`session_id` from stdin and exports it as the portable `REBAR_SESSION_ID`.

## What
Ship a small Claude Code SessionStart hook that reads `session_id` from the hook stdin
envelope and writes `REBAR_SESSION_ID=<id>` via the `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` mechanism, plus a
one-line install snippet. It ALSO exports the harness-provenance tag `AI_AGENT=claude-code`
(consumed by story c557's `claim_harness`), so a local-CLI claim records both the session id
AND its harness. Must be idempotent and fire-and-forget (no daemon, no shared state — see
epic gotcha G2).

## Scope
- IN: the hook script (exporting `REBAR_SESSION_ID` + `AI_AGENT`), install docs, a note that
  it must be session-scoped (not exported into a shell profile — gotcha G3), and handling of
  resume/continue double-fire (G1).
- OUT: Codex/Cursor shims (S6); the read/record core (S1-S3, S5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A SessionStart hook exports `REBAR_SESSION_ID` from the hook `session_id`.
- [ ] The same hook exports `AI_AGENT=claude-code` (the harness-provenance tag).
- [ ] With the hook installed, a local-CLI claim records `claimed_session` AND `claim_harness`
      (end-to-end).
- [ ] Hook is idempotent and does not error on resume/continue (documents the id-instability
      caveat G1).
- [ ] Install instructions documented; guidance to keep it session-scoped (not in profile).

## Depends on
- S1 (the `REBAR_SESSION_ID` contract) and S5 (the `AI_AGENT` -> `claim_harness` contract).
